abort.entry_not_loaded: The ConnectLife configuration entry is no longer loaded.
abort.issue_ignored: Ignored orphaned long-term statistics for ConnectLife sensors.

clear.description: This will permanently delete long-term statistics for {count} ConnectLife sensors. Continue?
clear.title: Clear orphaned long-term statistics

init.description:
{count} ConnectLife sensors have stored long-term statistics (LTS) but no longer report a state class. Home Assistant emits a separate repair for each one.

This is caused by a recent change in the integration: numeric properties no longer default to `state_class: measurement`, so state codes, mode flags, and setpoints are no longer recorded as LTS.

**Clear orphaned statistics** removes the stored LTS rows for all {count} sensors at once. The historical data for these sensors in Settings → Statistics will be deleted and cannot be recovered. The per-entity Home Assistant repairs will disappear the next time Home Assistant re-validates statistics — which happens when you open Developer Tools → Statistics. This bulk repair will not appear again on this configuration entry.

**Ignore** hides this bulk action and leaves the per-entity repairs in place — fix or ignore each one individually if you want finer-grained control over which sensors keep their history. You can come back to the bulk action later by selecting "Show ignored repairs" in the overflow menu on the top right of Settings → Repairs, then reopening this repair.

To review the individual sensor repairs before deciding, simply close this window — the bulk repair stays available as-is.

Note: this issue is marked critical only so it sorts above the per-entity recorder repairs. It is not urgent.
init.title: Orphaned long-term statistics ({count} sensors)

abort.issue_ignored: Genegeerd "{device_name} niet langer beschikbaar". Het apparaat en al zijn entiteiten zullen nog steeds in het register worden vermeld.
